discharge_obligations.role: identify the spatial domain and boundary structure on which integration by parts is applied
supply a representative or weak-spatial-regular payload for the restricted-continuation solution
justify that the weak gradient or flux field is admissible in the integration-by-parts formula
connect the spatial integration-by-parts step to the declared trace and boundary-flux pairing interface
prevent the spatial-regularity assumption from being marked discharged until the analytic payload is proved or certified

positive: Records the spatial-regularity integration-by-parts discharge lemma target after the time-regular energy discharge lemma.
Separates domain structure, spatial representative, weak-gradient or flux admissibility, and boundary pairing interface.
Makes explicit that the next regularity blocker is energy-density differentiability along restricted continuation.
negative: Does not prove spatial regularity for integration by parts.
Does not prove existence of a spatially regular representative.
Does not prove weak-gradient or flux admissibility.
Does not prove boundary trace or flux pairing.
Does not discharge the regularity-and-differentiation assumption group.
Does not prove the formal restricted-continuation theorem.
Does not close the full RR theorem.
